question: Will Donald Trump announce a new nominee for Chair of the Federal Reserve by January 9, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Trump administration announces a new nominee for Chair of the Federal Reserve by the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement within this market’s timeframe from Donald Trump or the Trump administration stating their intent to nominate a specific individual for Chair of the Federal Reserve will suffice to resolve this market, regardless of whether a formal nomination actually occurs. 

Qualifying announcements must explicitly present the relevant individual as the nominee or future nominee for Chair of the Federal Reserve. Announcements of acting or interim appointments, or announcements which merely reveal potential candidates, will not qualify. Media reports, speculation, or other unofficial information will not qualify.

A formal presidential nomination of an individual to be Chair of the Federal Reserve, made before the listed date, 11:59 PM ET, will immediately resolve this market to “Yes”. Formal nominations are defined as the submission of a nomination message to the U.S. Senate.

The resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from Donald Trump or the Trump administration and official information from the U.S. Senate.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - January 3, 4PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: 1H Spread: Rockets (-7.5)
description:
In the first half of the NBA game between Rockets and Nets, scheduled for January 1 at 6:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Rockets" if the Rockets are winning by 8 or more points at halftime.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Nets".

The result will be determined based on the score at halftime only.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Rockets vs. Nets
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for January 1 at 6:00PM ET:
If the Rockets win, the market will resolve to "Rockets".
If the Nets win, the market will resolve to "Nets".

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

description:

question: Jabari Smith Jr.: Assists Over 1.5
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for January 1 at 6:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Jabari Smith Jr. records more than 1.5 assists during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Jabari Smith Jr. records 1.5 assists or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

question: Dylan Sampson: Anytime Touchdown
description:
In the upcoming NFL game, scheduled for January 4 at 12:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Dylan Sampson scores a touchdown at any point during regulation or overtime.

This market will resolve to "No" if Dylan Sampson does not score a touchdown during regulation or overtime.

Qualifying Touchdowns:

- A rushing touchdown by the player

- A receiving touchdown caught by the player

- A special teams touchdown (e.g. punt/kick return)

Passing touchdowns do not qualify. Defensive touchdowns will be credited to the defensive unit, not individually credited to the player who scores.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BTC/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - January 2, 10:15PM-10:30PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Bitcoin price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BTC/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

description:

question: Brisbane International, Qualification: Anna Bondar vs Katie Volynets
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Anna Bondar and Katie Volynets in the Brisbane International, Qualification, scheduled for January 2 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Bondar' if Anna Bondar advances against Katie Volynets.

This market will resolve to 'Volynets' if Katie Volynets advances against Anna Bondar.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Big Bash League: Melbourne Stars vs Sydney Sixers (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Melbourne Stars and Sydney Sixers scheduled for January 8 2026 in Big Bash League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

description:

This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Melbourne Stars will be considered correct if  Melbourne Stars is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Sydney Sixers will be considered correct if Sydney Sixers is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.

description:

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

description:

This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Joburg Super Kings will be considered correct if  Joburg Super Kings is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Paarl Royals will be considered correct if Paarl Royals is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.

If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.

question: BPL: Sylhet Strikers vs Durdanto Dhaka (Game 1)
description:

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

question: BPL: Noakhali vs Durbar Rajshahi (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Noakhali and Durbar Rajshahi scheduled for January 8 2026 in BPL.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.icc-cricket.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

description:

question: Spread: Spurs (-6.5)
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for January 2 at 7:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Spurs" if the Spurs win the game by 7 or more points.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Pacers". If the game ends in a tie, this market will resolve to "Pacers".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Spurs vs. Pacers
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for January 2 at 7:00PM ET:
If the Spurs win, the market will resolve to "Spurs".
If the Pacers win, the market will resolve to "Pacers".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

question: Matas Buzelis: Points Over 16.5
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for January 2 at 8:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Matas Buzelis scores more than 16.5 points during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Matas Buzelis scores 16.5 points or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.
